name: David Brenner
blurb: After a career in television documentary work he turned to stand-up in 1969 and became the most frequent guest on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson in the 1970s and 1980s, with 158 appearances, and guest-hosted.
bio: David Brenner was an American stand-up comedian regarded as a pioneer of observational comedy. After a career in television documentary work he turned to stand-up in 1969 and became the most frequent guest on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson in the 1970s and 1980s, with 158 appearances, and guest-hosted for Carson 75 times. He released the comedy album Excuse Me, Are You Reading That Paper? and starred in four HBO specials.

timeline.ev: First paid stand-up gig at The Improv in New York
National television debut on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson
Begins guest-hosting The Tonight Show for Carson
Releases the comedy album Excuse Me, Are You Reading That Paper?
Hosts the syndicated late-night talk show Nightlife

accolades: Most frequent Tonight Show guest of the Carson era, with 158 appearances
Guest-hosted The Tonight Show for Carson 75 times
Broadcast Pioneers of Philadelphia Hall of Fame (2003)
